_schema._description: Canonical task model for the Study App backlog cockpit. Edit this header when introducing a new status / priority / sprint convention.

status.todo: Queued but not started. Owner assigned, spec ready to pick up.
status.in-progress: Actively being worked on now — open branch, draft, or PR.
status.blocked: Cannot move forward until an external dependency clears (see `blocked_by`).
status.done: Shipped and accepted. Acceptance checks pass; change is merged.

priority.P0: Critical — must ship before deadline; blocks other work.
priority.P1: High — committed to current scope; ship before deadline if humanly possible.
priority.P2: Medium — nice-to-have for current scope; first to be cut.

_schema.sprint_id_format: YYYY-Wnn (ISO week) or the literal string "backlog" for the unscheduled bucket. Must reference an entry in sprints.json.

tasks.title: Portal search: migrate from custom search-index.json + search.js to a maintained engine
tasks.summary: Replace the in-house BM25 search (assets_v2/ui-kit/components/search.js + assets/search-index.json) with a maintained static-friendly engine (Pagefind / Meilisearch / Typesense) and wire the index into make docs-fix so it never drifts again.
tasks.problem: Portal search today is a 418-line custom BM25 module plus a single-line prebuilt search-index.json that has to be rebuilt by hand. It has already drifted in prod (SEV-3 postmortem 2026-05-07 referenced in BL-053), there is no ranking-quality test, no typo tolerance, no synonyms, no analytics on zero-result queries, and every doc change risks stale results. The custom engine is now a maintenance liability with no owner — every portal page depends on it but nobody can debug ranking or extend it without reading the JS.
actions: Write a 1-page evaluation (Pagefind vs Meilisearch vs Typesense vs Algolia DocSearch) against criteria: static-friendly, self-hosted/no-CDN, multilingual, typo tolerance, build time on 585+ docs, index size, license, ranking quality
Pick one and record the decision as an ADR (extends ADR 0001 docs-as-code)
Prototype index build for the public + internal portal; hook it into `make docs-fix` and `make docs-check` so drift becomes a CI failure, not a postmortem
Replace `assets_v2/ui-kit/components/search.js` with the new engine's client (self-hosted bundle, no CDN), keep the existing topbar UI shell (`.docs-search`)
Preserve current UX: highlighting, prefix expansion, debounced input, keyboard nav, ≤12 results, mobile drawer parity
Add zero-result query logging (local, opt-in) so authoring gaps become visible
Remove `services/frontend/portal/assets/search-index.json` and the legacy `docs-search.js` once the new engine is live
Document the new search pipeline in `handbook/sa/authoring/` and link it from the docs-as-code overview
acceptance: Search index is rebuilt automatically by `make docs-fix`; `make docs-check` fails when the index is stale (regression test for the 2026-05-07 SEV-3)
All assets served same-origin — no third-party CDN, no telemetry leaving the box
Ranking-quality smoke test: a fixed query corpus (≥20 queries) returns the expected top-result page; lives in CI
Typo tolerance works for 1-edit-distance queries on indexed titles
Topbar search UX is at parity or better than today on desktop + mobile (no regression in keyboard nav, highlighting, result count)
Legacy `assets/search-index.json` and the custom BM25 `search.js` are deleted; nav-tree and runtime/shared README updated
ADR for the chosen engine is merged and linked from RFC 0004

tasks.title: QA foundation: tester space, process, and full testing pyramid (FE + BE)
tasks.summary: Stand up a dedicated QA workspace + canonical testing pyramid (unit/integration/e2e/visual) shared by frontend and backend.
tasks.problem: Today there is no QA hub, no agreed-on test layer ratios, and no single page that explains the difference between unit, integration, and e2e tests for this codebase. Coverage looks healthy but levels overlap and no one owns the gaps.
actions: Create internal/qa/{overview,layers,tooling,escalation}.html
Document the pyramid: target ratios per layer + when to add a test at each level
Pick canonical tooling per layer (pytest, Playwright, Hypothesis, k6) and pin versions
Wire QA hub into nav-tree-internal.json + cross-link from roles/qa
Add a roles/qa/radar entry per layer so blind spots are visible
acceptance: QA overview page renders and shows the full pyramid with current coverage per layer
Each layer has its own how-to page with a runnable example
Sidebar lists the QA hub; roles/qa cross-links to it

tasks.title: Integration tests against the database (CI)
tasks.summary: Run integration tests against a real database in CI, not a mock — catch migration + concurrency regressions before merge.
tasks.problem: Current backend tests stub the DB layer. Mock/prod divergence has bitten us; migrations pass locally but break on a fresh schema. We need a CI job that boots a throwaway Postgres and runs the full integration suite against it.
actions: Add a Postgres service container to .github/workflows/test.yml
Bootstrap schema via the same migration command used in prod
Mark integration tests with a pytest marker so they can run separately
Add a `make test-integration` target that mirrors CI locally
Document the test-data seeding contract
acceptance: CI job `test-integration` runs against Postgres on every PR
Migration regressions surface in CI (verified via a known-bad seed)
Local `make test-integration` matches CI behaviour

tasks.title: Working Swagger for frontend validation only
tasks.summary: Mount Swagger UI in the portal so frontend devs can validate request/response shapes without spinning up the backend.
tasks.problem: Frontend devs need a way to see live OpenAPI examples without running the backend. The current spec is static markdown; Swagger UI never finishes loading because of asset paths.
actions: Mount Swagger UI under /reference/api/swagger with the canonical openapi.yaml as source
Pin the UI version and host the bundle locally (no CDN)
Hide write operations — frontend validation only, no `Try it out`
Add a portal nav entry under Reference > API
acceptance: Swagger UI loads with all endpoints and example payloads
No console errors; assets served from same origin
Reference > API > Swagger link works from the sidebar

tasks.title: Requirements: traceability, stakeholder map, requirement conflicts
tasks.summary: Build a traceability matrix that ties every requirement to its stakeholder, source document, and test — and flag where requirements contradict.
tasks.problem: Requirements live scattered across RFCs, ADRs, and conversation threads. There is no single map showing who asked for what, where the requirement lives, or whether two stakeholders want incompatible things.
actions: Author internal/sa/requirements/traceability.html with a JSON-backed matrix
Add a stakeholder map page (org chart with goals + concerns per actor)
Document the conflict-resolution protocol (RACI + escalation path)
Cross-link each ADR to the requirement(s) it satisfies
acceptance: Matrix renders with at least 15 real requirements traced end-to-end
Every active RFC links back to its stakeholder + source requirement
Conflict page lists at least 3 documented trade-offs with resolutions

tasks.title: Read-model caching strategy and fallback policy
tasks.summary: Define the caching strategy for read models — TTLs, invalidation triggers, and what to serve when the cache is cold or stale.
tasks.problem: Read paths re-query the primary every time; latency is fine today but won't be at 10× load. There's no documented policy for when to cache, when to invalidate, or what to return when the cache is unavailable.
actions: Write an ADR covering: caching tiers, TTL per read model, invalidation hooks
Define the fallback policy: serve-stale vs hard-fail per endpoint
Add a `cache-policy.yaml` SoT consumed by the runtime
Add metrics: cache hit ratio, stale-serve count, fallback count
acceptance: ADR merged with stakeholder sign-off
Top 3 read endpoints honour the cache-policy.yaml at runtime
Dashboard shows hit ratio per endpoint

tasks.title: Async REST pattern: 202 + job status + pagination/sorting
tasks.summary: Codify the async-REST pattern: 202 Accepted + job-status endpoint + canonical pagination/sorting query params.
tasks.problem: Long-running endpoints currently block; some return 200 immediately and lie, some hang. Pagination/sorting params are ad-hoc per endpoint. Frontend can't predict how any new endpoint behaves.
actions: Write an ADR fixing the 202 → /jobs/{id} → 200 contract
Standardise pagination (`?page`, `?per_page`, `Link` header)
Standardise sorting (`?sort=field,-field2`)
Add OpenAPI examples for each pattern; update existing endpoints incrementally
acceptance: ADR merged
At least 2 long-running endpoints follow the new contract end-to-end
OpenAPI lint rule enforces pagination/sort param naming

tasks.title: DB capacity + SQL performance playbook + index audit
tasks.summary: Capture a database capacity model + SQL-perf playbook + index audit so query regressions don't ship silently.
tasks.problem: We have no model for `rows × access frequency × QPS` at expected growth, no runbook for diagnosing slow queries, and indexes were added reactively. The first scaling event will hurt.
actions: Build a capacity model (table + sparkline) for top-10 tables
Write internal/sre/db-perf-playbook.html: EXPLAIN walkthrough, common pitfalls
Run an index audit; document each non-PK index with the query that justifies it
Add a periodic `pg_stat_statements` snapshot job + dashboard
acceptance: Capacity model published with refresh recipe
Playbook has 3 worked examples (slow query → fixed)
Index audit doc lists every index + its justification

tasks.title: Concurrency scenarios: ACID/isolation guide + race tests
tasks.summary: Document the concurrency model (ACID + isolation level + locking) and back it with race-condition tests for the top 3 write paths.
tasks.problem: Isolation level isn't documented; nobody knows whether the system tolerates phantom reads. There are zero tests that intentionally interleave writes to verify safety. Race bugs will be hard to catch in prod.
actions: Write internal/architect/concurrency.html: ACID, isolation levels, our choices
Identify top 3 write paths most exposed to concurrency
Add race tests (pytest-asyncio gather pattern) for each
Document the locking primitives used (row locks, advisory locks)
acceptance: Concurrency guide merged
3 race tests in CI; deliberately failing variant proves they're real
Each top-3 write path has its lock strategy documented inline

tasks.title: Security hardening: PII classification, masking, retention
tasks.summary: Classify every PII field, define masking rules for logs/exports, and set retention windows per data class.
tasks.problem: PII isn't classified; logs may leak emails; we have no documented retention policy. Compliance review would fail today.
actions: Inventory PII fields across the schema; tag each (`pii.email`, `pii.phone`, …)
Add a masking layer for log output (structured logging hook)
Define retention windows per class; document in internal/sre/data-retention.html
Add a yearly purge job spec
acceptance: Field-level PII tags exist in the schema metadata
Logs sampled in CI contain no raw PII
Retention policy page published with sign-off block

tasks.title: Playwright visual-regression harness for component galleries
tasks.summary: Wire a Playwright visual-diff job over the UI Kit showcase pages so accidental visual regressions block merges.
tasks.problem: UI Kit changes ripple across 100+ pages. Today regressions are caught manually in the browser — usually after merge. We need pixel-diff coverage on the canonical showcase URLs.
actions: Add Playwright + visual-comparison dep with pinned versions
Identify the 20 most-load-bearing showcase pages
Take baseline snapshots in light + dark + mobile viewports
Add CI job that fails on > 0.1% diff with annotated artefacts
acceptance: `make visual-test` runs locally and produces diffs
CI fails on a deliberate visual regression
Baselines stored under tests/visual/baselines/ with refresh recipe

tasks.title: Public/internal documentation portal IA + governance consolidation (RFC 0004)
tasks.summary: Land RFC 0004: a single, audited IA for public + internal portals; consolidate governance pages under one nav root.
tasks.problem: Pages drifted into 4 parallel taxonomies (governance/, sa/, internal/, public/). Same content lives in 2-3 places; sidebars contradict each other. RFC 0004 is mid-flight (phases E + F left).
actions: Finish phase E: collapse governance/ into internal/governance/
Finish phase F: emit the consolidated nav-tree-internal.json from a generator
Verify with check_asset_refs.py — zero stale hrefs
Update sidebars for both portals to the new IA
Close out RFC 0004 with status=accepted + audit notes
acceptance: RFC 0004 marked accepted
Sidebar matches the canonical IA on both portals
check_asset_refs.py exits 0

tasks.title: Test pyramid: unit tests + property-based tests (Hypothesis)
tasks.summary: Backfill unit tests + add Hypothesis property tests for the pure-function layer (validators, parsers, scoring).
tasks.problem: Unit coverage is lopsided — service layer at 80%, pure utils at 30%. Pure functions are exactly where property-based tests pay off; we don't run any today.
actions: Identify pure-function modules (validators, parsers, scoring)
Add Hypothesis dep + write strategies per input type
Author property tests for the top 5 functions
Document the Hypothesis pattern in qa/layers/property-based.html
acceptance: Unit coverage on pure-function modules ≥ 90%
At least 5 property tests in CI
Pattern doc has a runnable example

tasks.title: E2E: HTTP scenarios against a running application
tasks.summary: Add HTTP-level e2e scenarios that hit a real running app (compose stack) covering the top 5 user journeys.
tasks.problem: Our highest-value tests today stop at the service layer. Nothing exercises the actual HTTP boundary including middleware, auth, and serialisation. Regressions in middleware are invisible.
actions: Add `make e2e-up` that boots the compose stack with seeded data
Pick 5 user journeys (login → action → result)
Write each as an HTTP-level scenario using httpx
Add CI job that runs e2e against the stack
acceptance: 5 e2e scenarios pass locally and in CI
Failure surfaces the offending HTTP step, not a stack trace
Seed data is reproducible across runs

tasks.title: Mutation testing pilot for critical rules
tasks.summary: Pilot mutation testing (mutmut) on the scoring + validation rules to measure how often tests would catch a real bug.
tasks.problem: Coverage % overstates safety — branches can be 100% covered by tests that assert nothing meaningful. Mutation testing exposes weak assertions.
actions: Add mutmut dev dependency
Run against scoring + validation modules only (scoped pilot)
Document mutation score; convert top survivors into test gaps
Decide: roll out or shelve based on signal/cost
acceptance: Pilot report with mutation score per module
At least 3 test gaps converted into new tests
Decision doc (adopt/shelve) merged

tasks.title: Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) and trace context
tasks.summary: Instrument services with OpenTelemetry so a request can be followed end-to-end across the HTTP → DB → cache hops.
tasks.problem: Debugging latency outliers means grepping logs across 3 services and guessing causality. Trace IDs aren't propagated. SRE work is way harder than it needs to be.
actions: Add opentelemetry-sdk + auto-instrumentation for FastAPI + SQLAlchemy
Propagate trace context via W3C traceparent header
Ship traces to a local Jaeger in compose; document setup
Add the trace ID to log output for correlation
acceptance: A single request shows a complete trace tree in Jaeger
Log lines carry the trace ID
SRE runbook page documents how to read a trace

tasks.title: Single source of truth for application / OpenAPI version metadata
tasks.summary: Pick one source of truth for version metadata (app version, OpenAPI version, image tag) and derive every other surface from it.
tasks.problem: Version drifts across pyproject.toml, openapi.yaml, the Docker tag, and the portal footer. Releases ship with mismatched numbers.
actions: Pick pyproject.toml as the canonical version source
Generate openapi.yaml `info.version` from it at build time
Stamp the Docker tag + portal footer from the same source
Add a pre-commit check that fails on drift
acceptance: All four surfaces show the same version at all times
Pre-commit fails if any drifts
Release runbook references the single source

tasks.title: CI: optional HTTP smoke or load-regression job
tasks.summary: Add an opt-in CI job that runs a 60-second HTTP smoke / load regression so latency regressions surface before release.
tasks.problem: Performance regressions are only caught when someone notices the UI feel slow. By then they're already in main. A short k6 burst on demand would prevent that.
actions: Author a small k6 script: 100 RPS for 60s against the top endpoints
Wire it as a manual-dispatch GH workflow
Publish a baseline; fail if p95 regresses > 25%
Document how to interpret the report
acceptance: Manual-dispatch workflow exists and runs to completion
Baseline checked into the repo
A deliberate slowdown causes the job to fail

tasks.title: Redis adoption: idempotency cache and short-lived platform data
tasks.summary: Introduce Redis for two specific use cases: idempotency keys and short-lived platform data (sessions, rate limits).
tasks.problem: We currently fake idempotency in Postgres which costs writes. Sessions also sit in Postgres. Both are textbook Redis use cases. Adopting it now keeps the schema clean later.
actions: Add Redis to compose + production manifest
Build an idempotency middleware backed by Redis SETNX + TTL
Migrate session store to Redis with a feature flag
Document Redis ops: persistence, eviction, backup
acceptance: Idempotency middleware live with a passing e2e test
Session store flag flips with zero downtime
Ops doc covers eviction + failover

tasks.title: Document versioning: Document Version field and change policy
tasks.summary: Add a `Document Version` field + change policy to every governance doc so reviewers know which revision they signed off on.
tasks.problem: Docs get edited in place without a version stamp; sign-off applies to 'whatever the page said at the time'. Hard to audit later.
actions: Define the policy: bump major on breaking, minor on additive
Add a docs-meta block to the canonical page template
Backfill the field on the top 20 governance pages
Add a pre-commit check that warns when a page changes without a bump
acceptance: Policy page merged
Top 20 governance pages carry a version stamp
Pre-commit warns on un-bumped edits

tasks.title: PDoc: navigation and unified application theme
tasks.summary: Theme the pdoc-generated API reference to match the portal, with a working sidebar that mirrors the module tree.
tasks.problem: pdoc output sits at /reference/api/python/ with default styling; the nav is broken and it looks like a different product. Users bounce.
actions: Author a pdoc HTML template that loads the portal stylesheet
Wire pdoc's nav into the portal sidebar at build time
Verify dark mode + mobile layout still work
Add a make target so the docs rebuild deterministically
acceptance: Generated reference looks visually identical to a hand-authored portal page
Sidebar nav navigates between modules
Dark mode + mobile tested

tasks.title: Modeling pack: UML Activity + State Machine + BPMN + RACI
tasks.summary: Ship a 'modeling pack' page: UML Activity, State Machine, BPMN, and RACI templates with the conventions we use.
tasks.problem: Different docs use different notations for the same things; readers waste time decoding. A canonical pack settles the question.
actions: Author internal/sa/modeling/pack.html with one canonical example per notation
Add PlantUML source for each example (no Mermaid)
Document when to reach for each notation
Cross-link from architect onboarding
acceptance: Modeling pack page published with 4 worked examples
Examples render via Kroki
Architect onboarding links to the pack

tasks.title: Estimation governance: plan/fact, forecasting, stakeholder SLA
tasks.summary: Document the estimation model: plan-vs-fact tracking, forecasting rules, and the SLA we commit to stakeholders.
tasks.problem: Estimates are guesses logged nowhere. There's no plan-vs-fact loop, so the next estimate is no better than the last. Stakeholders don't know what 'P1 in W21' actually promises.
actions: Author internal/manager/estimation/overview.html
Add a plan/fact table to each retrospective
Document the forecasting heuristic (rolling 4-sprint median)
Publish the stakeholder SLA matrix (P0..P3 → response time)
acceptance: Estimation overview page merged
Last 4 sprints have a plan/fact entry
SLA matrix is referenced from the public-facing roadmap

tasks.title: Feature flags and A/B experimentation baseline
tasks.summary: Pick a feature-flag system + write the experimentation baseline (hypothesis → metric → readout) we expect every A/B to follow.
tasks.problem: Risky changes either ship behind a manual env var or not at all. No team-wide pattern for A/B. The first real experiment will reinvent the wheel.
actions: Evaluate GrowthBook vs Unleash vs a homegrown YAML flag file
Pick one and document the integration
Write the experimentation template: hypothesis → metric → readout
Run one toy A/B to validate end-to-end
acceptance: ADR records the choice
Library wired into one endpoint with a flag
Toy A/B has a published readout

tasks.title: Backfill screen specs for top-3 unspec'd pages
tasks.summary: Write screen specs (purpose, states, copy, data deps, a11y) for the 3 most-trafficked pages that currently lack one.
tasks.problem: Some flagship pages have zero spec. Any redesign starts from reverse-engineering the live page — slow, error-prone, and erases the original intent.
actions: Identify the 3 unspec'd pages by traffic
For each: write purpose, states, copy, data deps, a11y notes, error cases
Cross-link to component explorer entries
Add to internal/sa/screens/ index
acceptance: 3 screen-spec pages published
Each includes wireframe + state table
screens/ index sorted by traffic

tasks.title: Per-component testing matrix (link spec to e2e/unit tests)
tasks.summary: Add a 'Testing' section to every UI Kit component page that links to the unit + e2e + visual tests covering it.
tasks.problem: Spec, code, and tests live in 3 places. Component reviewers can't tell what's actually tested without grepping. Coverage gaps are invisible.
actions: Add a `tests.json` per component listing covering test files
Render a 'Testing' section in the showcase template
Backfill the json for the top 20 components
Add a check that warns when a component has no tests linked
acceptance: Top 20 components show a populated Testing section
Check warns on components without test links
Section links resolve in CI

tasks.title: Distributed rate limiting with Redis (multi-instance)
tasks.summary: Replace per-instance in-memory rate limiting with a Redis-backed shared counter so limits actually hold under multi-instance deploys.
tasks.problem: Rate limit lives in process memory; with 3 instances a client gets 3× the limit. A misbehaving caller can still take us down.
actions: Pick the algorithm: token bucket vs sliding window
Implement on Redis with Lua for atomicity
Replace the in-memory middleware
Add a chaos test: 3 instances + 1 abusive client
acceptance: Aggregate limit holds across instances under load
Chaos test passes
Per-route limits documented in the API reference

tasks.title: Test data: seeds, fixtures, runbook
tasks.summary: Build a canonical test-data set + fixtures + the runbook for refreshing it after a schema change.
tasks.problem: Every test file rolls its own fixtures. Schema changes break tests in surprising places because nobody owns the seed data.
actions: Pick a fixture pattern (factory-boy or hand-rolled SQL)
Author a canonical seed set covering all entity types
Document the refresh runbook for schema changes
Migrate at least 3 noisy test files to the shared fixtures
acceptance: Shared fixtures used by ≥ 3 test files
Seed runbook merged
A deliberate schema change updates fixtures in one place

tasks.title: Feature flags (safe rollout)
tasks.summary: Production-grade feature-flag plumbing (separate from the A/B baseline) — percentage rollouts, kill switches, audit log.
tasks.problem: BL-038 picks the tech. This task is the production plumbing: percentage rollouts, kill switches, audit log of who flipped what when.
actions: Wire the chosen flag system into the runtime
Add a percentage-rollout primitive
Add an audit log of flag changes
Document the on-call kill-switch procedure
acceptance: Flag flips are logged with actor + timestamp
Percentage rollout works in a smoke test
On-call procedure documented + tested

tasks.title: Architecture fitness: enforced layer boundaries
tasks.summary: Add architecture-fitness checks (import-linter) that fail CI when a layer imports across the boundary.
tasks.problem: We have a layered architecture in our heads. Nothing enforces it. Domain code already reaches into the HTTP layer in 2 places.
actions: Add import-linter with rules per layer
Fix existing violations
Wire into pre-commit + CI
Document the layer contract
acceptance: import-linter passes on main
Deliberate violation fails CI
Layer contract page merged

tasks.title: Deprecation framework and migration guide template
tasks.summary: Adopt a deprecation framework: lifecycle stages, deprecation notice format, migration-guide template.
tasks.problem: Deprecations are announced inconsistently — sometimes in a release note, sometimes nowhere. Consumers find out by breakage. We need a predictable shape.
actions: Define lifecycle stages (active/deprecated/sunset/removed)
Author the migration-guide template
Add a docs-meta `deprecation` block
Backfill on 3 already-deprecated endpoints
acceptance: Framework page merged
3 deprecation notices in the new format
Template used for the next real migration

tasks.title: CI pipeline: quality gates, coverage, docs-check
tasks.summary: CI pipeline with quality gates: ruff, mypy, pytest coverage threshold, docs link-check.
tasks.problem: Every PR added drift — formatting, types, broken links. Without an enforced gate, reviewers became human linters.
actions: Wire ruff + mypy + pytest into GH Actions
Pin coverage threshold (75% min, fail-under)
Add docs-check (check_asset_refs.py)
Cache deps for speed
acceptance: All gates run on every PR
Coverage below threshold blocks merge
Pipeline completes in < 5 min on warm cache

tasks.title: SLOs / SLAs, error budget, monitoring alerts
tasks.summary: Defined the public SLOs/SLAs, an error budget, and the alert routing for budget burn.
tasks.problem: Nothing defined 'is the service healthy?'; alerts fired on CPU spikes, not user-visible degradation.
actions: Author SLO doc per service (latency, availability)
Define error budget + burn-rate alerts
Wire Prometheus rules + Alertmanager
Document the on-call response
acceptance: SLO page published per service
Burn-rate alerts fire on a synthetic load
On-call runbook merged

tasks.title: DB uniqueness + race safety + concurrency notes
tasks.summary: Added unique constraints + lock strategy for the duplicate-prone write paths; documented the concurrency notes.
tasks.problem: Two of the heavy write endpoints could create duplicates under contention; no constraint, no row lock.
actions: Identify duplicate-prone paths
Add unique constraints in a backward-compatible migration
Pick lock strategy per path (advisory vs row)
Document the choice
acceptance: Constraints in place; backfilled cleanly
Race test verifies no duplicate under load
Concurrency notes merged

tasks.title: Dependency security (pip-audit) + update policy
tasks.summary: pip-audit on every CI run + a stated update cadence + exception policy.
tasks.problem: Dependencies aged silently; CVEs were unknown unknowns. No agreed cadence.
actions: Add pip-audit to CI
Document the update cadence (monthly minor, weekly security)
Define exception policy for unfixable CVEs
Add owner per dep class
acceptance: pip-audit runs on every PR
Update cadence honoured for 2 cycles
Exception entries log the rationale

tasks.title: OpenAPI governance: lint + baseline + contract test
tasks.summary: Spectral lint + a baseline diff + contract test (Schemathesis) gating OpenAPI changes.
tasks.problem: OpenAPI spec drifted from the live API; reviewers had no signal that a change was breaking.
actions: Add Spectral with a custom ruleset
Snapshot the baseline; diff on PR
Wire Schemathesis as a contract test against the running app
Document the breaking-change escape hatch
acceptance: Lint blocks malformed specs
Diff comment posted on every PR touching openapi.yaml
Schemathesis passes on main

tasks.title: Continuous delivery: publish image to GHCR
tasks.summary: Tagged container images published to GHCR on every main merge + on tag.
tasks.problem: Releases were built locally and pushed manually; no immutable artefact, no clear rollback target.
actions: Add the build+push workflow
Tag with `main-<sha>` + semver on releases
Add image attestation (provenance)
Document the pull URL
acceptance: Image appears in GHCR for every merge
Semver tag exists for every release
Pull URL documented

tasks.title: Mobile docs UX (iPhone 17 Pro Max + adaptive layout)
tasks.summary: Adopted the unified mobile contract (≥1025 / 761-1024 / ≤760), removed viewport-conditional font sizes, tested on iPhone 17 Pro Max.
tasks.problem: Docs broke on phones — overlapping sidebars, tiny fonts under width-conditional media. No canonical breakpoint set.
actions: Pick canonical tiers and document in #mobile-contract
Strip width-conditional font-size rules
Build the mobile drawer that mirrors the desktop sidebar
Verify on real device + 3 viewport widths
acceptance: Mobile contract page is normative
No regressions across 3 widths
Real-device pass logged

tasks.title: Backlog page and layout (baseline)
tasks.summary: Original static backlog page — title, rows, status pills. Replaced by the cockpit in May 2026.
tasks.problem: No live backlog existed; tickets lived in chat threads.
actions: Author the static page with 50 rows
Wire into the sidebar
Add basic status pills
acceptance: Page exists

tasks.title: Load testing (k6 / Locust) + SLO validation
tasks.summary: k6 scripts + a load profile per critical endpoint validating the SLO under expected and 2× load.
tasks.problem: SLOs were asserted but never measured under load; no evidence we'd meet them.
actions: Pick k6 over Locust (single-binary, easier CI)

Run 1× + 2× expected load against staging
Publish results + recommendations
acceptance: k6 scripts checked in
Report published with p50/p95/p99
Recommendations turned into follow-up tickets

tasks.title: Changelog and release-time verification
tasks.summary: CHANGELOG.md + a release-time check that fails if the new version has no entry.
tasks.problem: Releases shipped without changelog entries; users couldn't tell what changed.
actions: Pick the Keep-A-Changelog format
Add a release-time check
Backfill entries for the last 3 releases
Document the contribution flow
acceptance: CHANGELOG.md follows the format
Release fails without an entry
Last 3 releases backfilled

tasks.title: Repository cleanup + dead code removal
tasks.summary: Removed dead modules, unused dependencies, stale dirs. Repo footprint down 18%.
tasks.problem: Repo accumulated orphan files from earlier experiments; navigating got harder every month.
actions: Run vulture for dead-code candidates
Audit deps with pip-tools' unused detector

Verify tests + docs still pass

Audit log entry per deletion batch

tasks.title: Structured logging (JSON) + correlation IDs
tasks.summary: Switched to structured JSON logging + per-request correlation ID propagated through middleware.
tasks.problem: Logs were unstructured strings; impossible to filter or correlate across services.
actions: Adopt structlog with JSON renderer
Generate correlation ID in middleware; pass via context
Include in every log call automatically
Update the SRE log query examples
acceptance: All log lines parse as JSON
Correlation ID present on every line of a request
Log query examples updated

tasks.title: Architectural narrative: single "How we got here" page
tasks.summary: Connect the 30 ADRs / 4 RFCs / 7 SRE explanations into a single arc — initial gamble, hit constraints, current shape, what we'd change today.
tasks.problem: Reader sees decisions but no arc. Closes Vol.2 Architect's-Bar critical gap #1 — without a narrative, ADR coverage reads as notes, not as a history.
actions: Author explanation/architecture-narrative.html (8-12 KB, ~30 min read)
Tell the arc: starting hypothesis → forces that bent it → current shape → deliberate omissions
Wire into Explanation hub + sidebar; surface from cold-start.html#architect
acceptance: Page renders, ≥ 20 KB body, 6-point story
Each load-bearing decision links to its ADR
Cold-start architect path lists it as read #1

tasks.title: Postmortem corpus: 3 retroactive postmortems + index summary
tasks.summary: Replace "no postmortems on file yet" with 3 written postmortems covering real internal incidents (OpenAPI drift, SQLite lock burst, docs-fix index drift).
tasks.problem: Zero closed postmortems is the loudest operational-immaturity signal. Closes Vol.2 critical gap #2.
actions: Write 2026-03-14 OpenAPI baseline drift (SEV-3)
Write 2026-04-02 SQLite write-lock burst (SEV-2)
Write 2026-05-07 docs-fix search-index drift (SEV-3)
Update how-to/incidents/postmortems/index.html with a Date/Severity/Title/Takeaway table
acceptance: 3 postmortem files exist with timeline, contributing factors, SMART action items
Index page lists them with one-line takeaways
Each postmortem cross-linked from changed surfaces

tasks.title: Senior architect cold-start read path (15 curated reads)
tasks.summary: Curated per-persona reading order for a staff+ architect — 15 pages, ordered, each with "why read" / "what you learn".
tasks.problem: Reader-personas were declared but the architect had no specific path. Closes Vol.2 critical gap #3.
actions: Add #architect anchored section to onboarding/cold-start.html
Sequence: narrative → reader-personas → diataxis → topology → domain-model → spine-matrix → workflows → 4 key ADRs → SLOs → observability → postmortem → traceability
Each step: one-line why + duration estimate
acceptance: cold-start.html#architect lists 15 reads in correct order
Total budget ≤ 1-2 working days
All 15 targets exist and resolve

tasks.title: Threat model: STRIDE + adversary tiers + per-component coverage
tasks.summary: Single reference page enumerating attacks per STRIDE category against api / database / integrator, with mitigation status and adversary-tier scoping.
tasks.problem: ADR 0005 sets security defaults but without a threat model security choices look ad-hoc. Closes Vol.2 high-severity gap #4.
actions: Author handbook/security/threat-model.html
Decompose into STRIDE × component matrix with covered/partial/open status
Define adversary tiers (T1 state, T2 motivated, T3 script-kiddie) and which we're in scope for
Cross-link from ADR 0005 and runbook 0006
acceptance: Threat-model page renders
Every STRIDE category × component cell has a status
ADR 0005 references the page

tasks.title: Capacity snapshot: real RPS / latency numbers from synthetic drill
tasks.summary: Convert the capacity-drill tutorial output into a reference page with measured RPS, p50/p95/p99, saturation signal, and headroom.
tasks.problem: SLOs had numbers; capacity didn't. Closes Vol.2 high-severity gap #5 — turns aspirational targets into measured budgets.
actions: Run synthetic load test (k6) and record p50/p95/p99 + RPS-at-saturation
Author handbook/capacity-snapshot.html with the numbers + saturation signal (SQLITE_BUSY)
Cite back to the 2026-04-02 postmortem as evidence
Cross-link from handbook/sre/slos.html
acceptance: Capacity page lists real RPS + latency percentiles
Saturation signal named explicitly
Linked from SLOs Explanation

tasks.title: Data invariants reference: enumerated I-NN promises
tasks.summary: Single reference page listing all data invariants (I-01, I-02, …) with enforcement layer (FK / UNIQUE / CHECK / app-layer) and breakage symptom.
tasks.problem: Schemas are described per-resource but nothing enumerates the invariants reviewers and tests can rely on. Closes Vol.2 high-severity gap #6.
actions: Author handbook/data/invariants.html with numbered I-NN entries
For each: statement, enforcement layer, breakage signal
Cross-link from data-model + spec template
acceptance: Page lists ≥ 12 invariants
Each invariant has enforcement column
Each invariant has breakage column

tasks.title: ADR alternatives audit pass — retrofit 29 remaining ADRs
tasks.summary: Add a mandatory "Alternatives considered" block (≥ 2 honest rejected options with one-line rationale) to every ADR that lacks one.
tasks.problem: Audit tracker shows 1 STRONG / 13 WEAK / 16 MISSING out of 30 ADRs. Closes Vol.2 high-severity gap #7 — without honest rejections an ADR loses value within a year.
actions: ADR template updated with mandatory MIN 2 block (DONE)
alternatives-audit.html tracker (DONE) — drives the work
Retrofit 16 MISSING ADRs first (highest impact)
Re-audit 13 WEAK ADRs and upgrade to STRONG
Update tracker statuses as each ADR ships
acceptance: Every ADR has h2#alternatives with ≥ 2 rejected alternatives
alternatives-audit.html shows 30 / 30 STRONG
Each rejected has a one-line "because" clause

tasks.title: Lessons from production: cross-cutting patterns from real postmortems
tasks.summary: Extract recurring patterns from the postmortem corpus into a single Explanation page so SRE concepts stop reading like a textbook recap.
tasks.problem: observability / logging / monitoring read like SRE-Book summaries without team-specific insight. Closes Vol.2 medium-severity gap #8.
actions: Author handbook/sre/lessons-from-production.html
Extract patterns from the 3 postmortems (client behaviour as contract, etc.)
Cross-link from observability / logging / monitoring
acceptance: Lessons page exists with ≥ 3 named patterns
Each pattern cites at least one postmortem
Linked from 3 SRE Explanation pages

tasks.title: Tradeoffs sweep across Explanation pages
tasks.summary: Add a §Tradeoffs section to every Explanation page so the portal reads as opinion-from-experience, not as a textbook recap.
tasks.problem: Only test-pyramid had a Tradeoffs section. Closes Vol.2 high-severity gap #7 extension.
actions: Pass over all explanation/**/*.html files
Add an h2#tradeoffs block to each: pattern / anti-pattern / what we chose / what we accept losing
acceptance: ≥ 14 Explanation pages carry id="tradeoffs"
Each section names ≥ 1 rejected alternative
No anti-list-style "X is best" claims left without compromise context

tasks.title: IA scalability policy + project-wide glossary
tasks.summary: Document when a new top-level section is allowed and consolidate project-specific terms (conspectus, ETR, review slot, attempt, system-user) in one glossary.

actions: Author explanation/ia-scalability.html with the slicing-dimension policy
Author onboarding/glossary.html covering project-specific domain terms
Cross-link from spine-matrix and topology
acceptance: IA-scalability page enumerates allowed top-level buckets + when to add one
Glossary lists ≥ 20 project-specific terms
Both pages wired into sidebar

tasks.title: Feedback widget: page-level "was this useful?"
tasks.summary: Add a per-page feedback widget (yes/no + optional comment) so doc quality stops being measured by gut feel.
tasks.problem: DX-audit 2026-05-25 rated Feedback & CI 5/10 (weakest axis). Without a feedback signal we cannot tell which pages actually help vs which only look polished.
actions: Design a kit component in assets_v2/ui-kit/components/feedback-widget.css + entry.js
Decide ingest target (initially: localStorage + downloadable JSON; later: an endpoint)
Inject into the standard page footer template
Document the privacy posture (no PII, no identifiers)
acceptance: Widget renders on every internal portal page
Yes/no plus optional 200-char comment captured
Submissions are retrievable for review
Privacy note on the widget itself

tasks.title: "Edit on GitHub" link in page footer
tasks.summary: Add a footer link that deep-links to the current page's source file on GitHub so contributors can propose edits without spelunking the repo.
tasks.problem: DX-audit row 20 rated this 3/10 — the friction of finding a page's source kills small fixes. One template change closes it.
actions: Compute the page's path relative to repo root
Build the github.com/.../edit/main/<path> URL in the layout template
Add the link to the existing page footer (consistent on every page)
acceptance: Every internal portal page renders an "Edit on GitHub" link
Clicking it lands on the right file in the repo
Source repo URL is derived from a single config var

tasks.title: Service folder split
tasks.summary: Realign the repo to the service boundaries already documented in the internal catalog: move app/ → services/api/, alembic/ → services/api/, ops/prometheus + ops/grafana → services/monitoring/, the root Dockerfile and the logging compose into the owning service. Land Phase 1 of ADR 0028 (pure folder split, zero behaviour change).
tasks.problem: The repo root mixes the FastAPI app (app/), the database migrations (alembic/, alembic.ini), the monitoring stack (ops/prometheus/, ops/grafana/), a standalone observability compose (docker-compose.logging.yml), and the API Dockerfile — all sitting next to the docs (services/portal/). The internal service catalog at services/portal/internal/services/ already lists api · datastore · monitoring · portal · ui-kit as distinct logical services with per-service catalog-info.yaml + architecture page, but the actual code layout never caught up. Two costs: (1) ownership is fuzzy — there is no folder-as-boundary signal a reviewer can read; (2) per-service Dockerfiles / per-service CI jobs / per-service CODEOWNERS are impossible until the split lands. ADR 0028 has been Proposed since 2026-05-07 with Phase 1 «pure rename, no behaviour change» blocked on no one cutting the move.
actions: Move app/ → services/api/app/; alembic/ + alembic.ini → services/api/{alembic,alembic.ini}
Move ops/prometheus/, ops/grafana/ → services/monitoring/{prometheus,grafana}/; fold docker-compose.logging.yml into services/monitoring/
Move the root Dockerfile → services/api/Dockerfile (Phase 2 ratifies per-service Dockerfiles, but the file already only builds the API)
Update Makefile targets, .pre-commit-config.yaml file patterns, .github/workflows/*.yml paths, pyproject.toml (ruff/pytest/mypy roots), alembic.ini script_location
Add a CODEOWNERS file at repo root mapping each service path to @ivan — the «day-one investment» called out in ADR 0028's Would-we-do-this-again section
Update services/portal/internal/services/{api,monitoring,datastore}/architecture.html + each catalog-info.yaml so the documented paths match reality
Flip ADR 0028 status Proposed → Accepted with a history row noting Phase 1 implementation; mark Phases 2-4 as the remaining follow-ups
One-shot path-rewrite script scripts/migrate_phase1_paths.py for any cross-refs that miss bulk sed; delete after the merge
acceptance: make verify is green after the move — pure rename, zero behaviour change
app/, alembic/, ops/ no longer exist at repo root; all three live under services/<svc>/
CODEOWNERS file present and matched by GitHub (`gh api repos/iboiarkin96/etr_study_api/codeowners/errors` returns []`)
ADR 0028 status pill flips to Accepted; history row records the date and links the merge PR
Internal catalog architecture pages cite the new paths verbatim (no surviving references to `app/` or `ops/` at repo root)
CI build still produces the API image at the same tag; existing GHCR push job paths updated

tasks.title: Portal staging stand on GitHub Pages (dual-repo pattern)
tasks.summary: Stand up a pre-release Pages stand for the portal using the dual-repo pattern fixed by ADR 0034: source repo <app> keeps publishing prod from main; a new public mirror repo <app>-staging is CI-populated from the staging branch and gets its own Pages URL, cache, and 404. Procedure for releasing through staging lives at services/portal/how-to/release-to-staging.
tasks.problem: Today the portal publishes straight from main to GitHub Pages with no intermediate environment. A class of bugs (Pagefind bundle fetch path, UI Kit asset references via nav-tree, cross-page navigation) only manifests under a real Pages domain at the right base path — and the prod URL is the wrong place to discover them. GitHub Pages caps each repo at one site, so a second environment requires either a subfolder (shared 404/cache/CNAME, race conditions, leaky), a vendor outside GitHub (Cloudflare/Netlify/Vercel — splits the deploy story, rejected by ADR 0034), or a mirror repo (chosen). The decision is documented; the stand itself is not yet provisioned and the CI workflows are not yet wired.
actions: W0 — Create the public repo <owner>/<app>-staging; enable Pages → Source = Deploy from a branch, branch = main, folder = /

W0 — Create a GitHub App with contents:write scoped to <app>-staging; install it on the owner; store STAGING_DEPLOY_APP_ID + STAGING_DEPLOY_APP_KEY as Actions secrets on <app> (PAT fallback: STAGING_DEPLOY_TOKEN)
W1 — Land .github/workflows/deploy-prod.yml on the official actions/upload-pages-artifact + actions/deploy-pages path
W1 — Land .github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml using peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3 with external_repository, publish_branch: main, force_orphan: true
W1 — Parameterise the portal build via BASE_URL (vite or equivalent); verify both /<app>/ and /<app>-staging/ produce a working build locally
W2 — Add branch-protection rules on main and staging in <app> (require PR + 1 review + green status checks; disallow force-push)
W2 — Declare GitHub Environments production and staging on <app> with environment-scoped secrets; add a Required reviewer to the production environment
W2 — Inject <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> into the staging build at compile time
Flip ADR 0034 status Proposed → Accepted with a history row noting W0–W2 implementation; advance the Implementation lifecycle stage In progress → Done
acceptance: After W0: <owner>.github.io/<app>-staging/ serves a placeholder; Pages settings page reports «Your site is live»
After W1: a commit on staging updates the staging URL within ~60 s and the prod URL is unchanged; a commit on main updates prod and staging is unchanged
After W1: two concurrent pushes (one to main, one to staging) both succeed — neither cancels the other (concurrency: pages-deploy-<env>, cancel-in-progress: false)
After W1: zero failed asset loads on either URL when filtering the network panel to 404s
After W2: a force-push to main is rejected by branch protection; a deploy to the production environment pauses until the Required reviewer approves explicitly
ADR 0034 status pill flips Proposed → Accepted; index tickers Proposed 4→3 / Accepted 29→30; lifecycle popover is-current moves to Accepted / Done
